channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two People Arrested Over Investigation Into Dezi Freeman's Movements
item.description: Two people were arrested in Victoria’s northeast on Saturday as part of an ongoing investigation into the movements of Dezi Freeman, a fugitive who was shot dead earlier this week. The pair, a man and...

item.title: Lerato Kganyago and Lawrence Maleka set to host the 2026 Metro FM Awards
item.description: The 2026 Metro FM Awards, one of South Africa's most anticipated events, will be hosted by Lerato Kganyago and Lawrence Maleka. For the first time, the prestigious award ceremony will take place at Mo...

item.title: Improving Health and Livability in African Cities
item.description: Dis wata, African cities dey face bare challenges, but dem still get capacity to function well if correct systems dey. The book 'Urban Health in Africa' explore how rapid urbanisation dey affect publi...

item.title: Researchers Develop Highly Energy-Efficient AI System
item.description: Researchers have announced a breakthrough in artificial intelligence, creating a new nanoelectronic device that significantly improves energy efficiency. The device demonstrates machine-learning class...

item.title: Karnataka's EV Charging Network Expands to 2,039 Stations, Securing Second Position Nationwide
item.description: Karnataka has become the second-largest hub for Electric Vehicle (EV) public charging stations in India, with 2,039 operational units installed by oil marketing companies (OMCs). This achievement posi...

item.title: Kamene Goro Shares Bold Emails from Admirers Despite Being Married
item.description: Veteran radio presenter Kamene Goro, well-known in Kenya, recently stirred online conversations after sharing private emails she received from men expressing romantic interest. Kamene, who is happily ...

item.title: Global Tensions, Economic Warnings, and Canada's Labour Market: Key Business Updates
item.description: This week's business briefing highlights several key developments impacting the global economy and Canada. Rising tensions between the U.S. and Iran are causing volatility in oil markets, with crude p...

item.title: Former Energy Secretary says global oil market recovery will remain slow even after Middle East conflict ends
item.description: In an interview reported by ABS-CBN News, a former Philippine Energy Secretary warned that global oil prices would not quickly return to their pre-crisis levels even if the ongoing war in the Middle E...

item.title: Young Girl Creates Storytelling Trail with Trees in Hackney Marshes
item.description: An 11-year-old girl named Niovi has been transforming the trees along the River Lea in Hackney Marshes into imaginative characters. Over the past year, she has been creating whimsical backstories for ...

item.title: Gun battle near Israeli consulate in Istanbul leaves one dead and two wounded
item.description: A dramatic shootout took place on Tuesday outside the building housing the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, leaving one suspected attacker dead and two others wounded, according to Turkish authorities a...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs Coach Focuses on Maintaining Momentum After Third Straight Win
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs co-coach Khalil Ben Youssef has emphasized the importance of keeping their winning momentum after securing their third consecutive league victory, a 3-1 win over Orbit College in Gqeberh...

item.title: Pakistan Army Condemns Attacks on Saudi Energy Sites, Urges Restraint and Diplomacy
item.description: The Pakistani Army’s senior command has strongly condemned recent attacks on Saudi Arabia’s energy infrastructure, describing them as an unnecessary escalation amid the ongoing US-Israeli conflict wit...

item.title: Benni McCarthy Aims to Recruit German-Based Players with Kenyan Roots for Harambee Stars
item.description: Harambee Stars head coach Benni McCarthy has unveiled a fresh plan to bring in footballers of Kenyan heritage who currently play in Germany’s Bundesliga and lower tiers. The South African tactician, w...

item.title: Chinese Delegation Supports Tazara Railway Rehabilitation
item.description: The long-awaited revitalisation of the Tanzania-Zambia Railway Authority (Tazara) has gained new momentum after senior officials from China Railway Construction Corporation (CRCC) visited Dar es Salaa...

item.title: China Conducts Maiden Flight of Hydrogen-Powered Megawatt Turboprop Engine
item.description: China's Aero Engine Corporation (AECC) has successfully conducted the first flight of the AEP100, a megawatt-class hydrogen-fueled turboprop engine. The test was carried out on a 7.5-ton unmanned carg...

item.title: Pakistan Army condemns attacks on Saudi industrial sites, urges diplomatic resolution
item.description: The Pakistan Army’s senior leadership has issued a strong condemnation of recent assaults targeting Saudi Arabia’s petrochemical and industrial facilities. In a statement released by the Inter-Service...

item.title: Remembering Albert Mazibuko: Guardian of Isicathamiya Traditions
item.description: Albert Mazibuko, a key member of Ladysmith Black Mambazo, passed away on 5 April 2026 at the age of 77. Known for his gentle spirit and wisdom, Mazibuko played a central role in preserving and nurturi...

item.title: ‘Dhurandhar’ movie’s political impact spreads across India, from UP posters to Modi’s Kerala speech
item.description: The movie 'Dhurandhar' has become a major political tool across India since its release in December 2025, with the sequel intensifying its impact. The film, which has broken box office records, is bei...

item.title: Nearly 91 lakh voters deleted from Bengal electoral rolls after revision process
item.description: Nearly 91 lakh voters have been struck off West Bengal’s electoral lists as part of a special intensive revision carried out by the Election Commission before the upcoming Assembly elections. Accordin...

item.title: UK Government to Cap Interest Rates on Plan 2 and 3 Student Loans at 6%
item.description: The UK government has announced that interest rates on Plan 2 and Plan 3 student loans in England and Wales will be capped at 6% starting in September for one year. This measure aims to protect studen...
